suicidal_ideation.title: Suicidal ideation response
steps: Ask directly and calmly about suicidal thoughts — plain language, no euphemisms.
Assess plan, means, and timeline; imminence changes everything.
Collaboratively increase distance from means ("would you be willing to…").
Co-create one concrete safety step for tonight, however small.
If risk is imminent, escalate per your organisation's protocol — never carry it alone.
Debrief with your team afterwards; log per your documentation policy.

self_harm.title: Self-harm response
steps: Screen for injuries needing medical attention right now.
Validate without judgment; self-harm is a coping strategy, not attention-seeking.
Explore what the self-harm helps them get through.
Offer harm-reduction and delay techniques for this wave only.
Gently screen for suicidal intent — related but distinct.

domestic_violence.title: Domestic violence response
steps: Confirm they are safe to talk right now; agree on short answers or a stop signal.
Believe and validate explicitly; never question their account.
Support their autonomy — do not push them to leave; leaving is the highest-risk moment.
Offer to build a safety plan: documents, money, a place to go, a code word.
Share specialised DV referral resources; confirm it is safe for them to keep them.

substance_use.title: Substance use response
steps: Screen for overdose risk first: what, how much, when, mixed with what.
If overdose is possible, escalate to emergency services per protocol immediately.
Stay nonjudgmental — shame drives disconnection.
Anchor on the immediate: who can be with them tonight, what has helped before.
Offer specialised referral for follow-up support.

panic_anxiety.title: Panic / acute anxiety response
steps: Grounding before problem-solving: 5-4-3-2-1 senses, feet on floor.
Guide slow breathing, emphasising the long exhale.
Keep your messages short, slow, and steady — your pace is the intervention.
Name the pattern: panic peaks and passes; the goal is the next few minutes.
Once settled, gently explore triggers and next-step support.

grief_loss.title: Grief and loss response
steps: Presence over solutions; do not rush toward silver linings.
Invite, never require: "Would you like to tell me about them?"
Normalise the full range — numbness, anger, guilt, relief.
Watch for hopelessness bleeding into suicidal thinking; screen gently.
Offer continuing support options before closing.

general_distress.title: General distress response
steps: Open questions and reflective listening until the core concern surfaces.
Validate the feeling before addressing any facts.
Re-classify as the situation clarifies; protocols can change mid-conversation.
Close with one concrete, self-chosen next step.

notice: Curated directory of publicly listed helplines. Volunteers should verify numbers against current local directories before sharing.
